1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

9	# Genus-`0` base objects: `ℙ¹`, `Ga`, `Gm`, and the `Gm`-scaling action `σ_×`
10	
11	This file ships the concrete genus-`0` base-case infrastructure (iter-165 lane) that
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes via
13	the **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Proposition 3.10): the total
14	scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
15	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`); the `W`-axis collapses at the scaling fixed point
16	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, giving `f(λx) = f(x)`; density of `𝔾_m ⊆ ℙ¹` plus `A` separated
17	(`ext_of_eqOnOpen`) force `f` constant. NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
18	`Hom(𝔾_a, A) = 0`, char-general. See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`
19	(`def:genus0_base_objects`, `def:gaTranslationP1`).
20	
21	The file is **upstream** of `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` (the mathlib-analogist
22	`gm-scaling-p1` D4 verdict: split into a focused file per scheme construction, mirroring
23	the precedent of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.{Smooth,Abelian}`). The AVR.lean refactor +
24	proof of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` is iter-166's lane and does NOT happen here.
25	
26	## The four declarations
27	
28	1. `ProjectiveLineBar` — the projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as `Proj 𝒜` of
29	   the standard ℕ-graded `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, viewed as an object of
30	   `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. `IsProper` is FREE from
31	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` (the algebra is finite type).
32	   `GeometricallyIrreducible` and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` are project-side sub-builds
33	   (Mathlib does not ship these for `Proj`), left as scaffold `sorry`s for iter-166+.
34	
35	2. `Ga` — the additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
36	   `(AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).asOver _`. `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
37	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the additive-group functor
38	   `T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; `Smooth` is FREE from
39	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
40	
41	3. `Gm` — the multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
42	   `(Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))).asOver _`
43	   (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`, AFFINE — NOT the basic-open path). `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
44	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the units functor
45	   `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`; `Smooth` is FREE from
46	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
47	
48	4. `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar` — the bare scaling
49	   morphism `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, with companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposing
50	   the load-bearing fixed-point property at `0 ∈ ℙ¹` that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
51	   (Cor 1.5) needs.
52	
53	The blueprint chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` covers this file via
54	`% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean
55	AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`.
56	-/
57	
58	set_option autoImplicit false
59	set_option linter.style.setOption false
60	
61	universe u
62	
63	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
70	
71	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
72	
73	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
74	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
75	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
76	
77	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
78	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
80	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
81	
82	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
83	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
84	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
85	
86	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
87	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
88	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
89	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
90	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
91	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
92	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
93	    Algebra kbar
94	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
95	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
96	
97	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
98	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
99	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
100	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).

100	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
101	
102	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
103	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
104	irreducibility instances. -/
105	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
106	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
107	
108	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
109	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
110	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
111	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
112	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
113	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
114	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
115	
116	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
117	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
118	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
119	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
120	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
121	
122	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
123	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
124	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
125	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
126	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
127	
128	The chain:
129	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
130	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
131	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
132	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
133	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
134	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
135	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
136	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
137	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
138	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
139	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
140	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
141	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
142	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
143	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
144	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
145	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
146	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
147	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
148	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
149	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
150	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
151	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
152	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
153	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
154	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
155	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
156	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
157	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
158	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
159	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
160	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
161	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
162	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
163	    · intro x y h
164	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
165	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
166	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
167	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
168	      apply Subtype.ext
169	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
170	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
171	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
172	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
173	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
174	      exact key
175	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
176	  -- proper.
177	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
178	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
179	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
180	    exact hbij
181	  infer_instance
182	
183	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
184	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
185	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
186	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
187	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
188	  sorry
189	
190	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
191	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
192	acceptable for iter-165). -/
193	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
194	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
195	  sorry
196	
197	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
198	
199	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
200	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
201	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
202	
203	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
204	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
205	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
206	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
207	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
208	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
209	  let f : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
210	  let m : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
211	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (m := m) f
212	    (fun i ↦ by
213	      fin_cases i <;> simp [f, m, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
214	        MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X])
215	    (fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;> simp [m])
216	    (by
217	      classical
218	      intro p hp
219	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
220	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
221	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
222	        have := hp
223	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
224	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
225	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
226	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
227	        exact this
228	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
229	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
230	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
231	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
232	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
233	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
234	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
235	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
236	        by_contra h
237	        push Not at h
238	        apply hd_ne
239	        ext k
240	        fin_cases k
241	        · simpa using h.1
242	        · simpa using h.2
243	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
244	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
245	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
246	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
247	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
248	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
249	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
250	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
251	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
252	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
253	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
254	
255	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
256	
257	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
258	private def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
259	  | 0 => 1
260	  | 1 => 0
261	
262	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
263	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
264	
265	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
266	  fin_cases i <;> decide
267	
268	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
269	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
270	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
271	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
272	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
273	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
274	
275	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
276	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
277	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
278	
279	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
280	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
281	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
282	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
283	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
284	
285	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
286	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
287	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
288	
289	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
290	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
291	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
292	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
293	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
294	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
295	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
296	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
297	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
298	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
299	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
300	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
301	
302	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
303	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
304	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
305	    kbar →+*
306	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
307	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
308	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
309	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
310	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
311	
312	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
313	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
314	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
315	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
316	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
317	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
318	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
319	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
320	    (fun _ : Unit =>
321	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
322	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
323	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
324	
325	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
326	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
327	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
328	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
329	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
330	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
331	  · intro r
332	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
333	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
334	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
335	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
336	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
337	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
338	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
339	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
340	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
341	      MvPolynomial.C r
342	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
343	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
344	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
345	  · intro _
346	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
347	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
348	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
349	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
350	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
351	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
352	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
353	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
354	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
355	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
356	      MvPolynomial.X ()
357	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
358	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
359	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
360	
361	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
362	
363	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
364	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
365	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
366	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
367	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
368	
369	Proof structure (iter-172):
370	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
371	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
372	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
373	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
374	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
375	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
376	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
377	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
378	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
379	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
380	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
381	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
382	  classical
383	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
384	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
385	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
386	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
387	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
388	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
389	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
390	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
391	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
392	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
393	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
394	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
395	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
396	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
397	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
398	    apply top_unique
399	    intro p _

400	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
401	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
402	      intro r
403	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
404	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
405	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
406	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
407	      rw [← h]
408	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
409	    · -- add case
410	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
411	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
412	    · -- mul_X case
413	      intro p₁ j hp₁
414	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
415	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
416	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
417	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
418	    v hx dv hxd
419	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
420	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
421	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
422	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
423	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
424	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
425	    apply Subtype.ext
426	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
427	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
428	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
429	    exact key
430	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
431	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
432	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
433	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
434	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
435	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
436	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
437	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
438	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
439	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
440	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
441	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
442	      have h := hai
443	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
444	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
445	      omega
446	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
447	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
448	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
449	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
450	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
451	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
452	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
453	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
454	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
455	    simp only [pow_one]
456	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
457	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
458	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
459	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
460	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
461	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
462	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
463	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
464	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
465	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
466	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
467	      subst heq_i
468	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
469	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
470	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
471	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
472	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
473	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
474	      subst heq_i
475	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
476	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
477	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
478	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
479	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
480	  intro y
481	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
482	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
483	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
484	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
485	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
486	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
487	    rw [htop]; trivial
488	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
489	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
490	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
491	  · -- mem case
492	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
493	    have hgen :=
494	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
495	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
496	    rw [hgen]
497	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
498	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
499	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
500	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
501	    rw [map_pow, hX]
502	  · -- algebraMap case
503	    intro r
504	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
505	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
506	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
507	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
508	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
509	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
510	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
511	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
512	    rw [hr₀]
513	    rfl
514	  · -- add case
515	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
516	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
517	  · -- mul case
518	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
519	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
520	
521	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
522	
523	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
524	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
525	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
526	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
527	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
528	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
529	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
530	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
531	      RingHom.id _ := by
532	  ext x
533	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
534	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
535	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
536	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
537	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
538	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
539	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
540	
541	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
542	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
543	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
544	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
545	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
546	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
547	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
548	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
549	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
550	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
551	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
552	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
553	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
554	
555	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
556	
557	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
558	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
559	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
560	
561	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
562	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
563	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
564	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
565	
566	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
567	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
568	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
569	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
570	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
571	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
572	
573	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
574	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
575	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
576	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
577	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
578	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
579	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
580	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
581	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
582	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
583	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
584	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
585	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
586	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
587	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
588	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
589	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
590	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
591	    exact hi.map _
592	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
593	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
594	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
595	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
596	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
597	
598	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
599	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
600	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
601	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
602	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
603	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
604	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
605	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
606	  Over.homMk
607	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
608	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
609	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
610	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
611	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
612	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
613	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
614	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
615	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
616	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
617	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
618	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
619	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
620	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
621	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
622	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
623	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
624	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
625	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
626	      ext r
627	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
628	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
629	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
630	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
631	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
632	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
633	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
634	
635	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
636	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
637	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
638	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
639	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
640	
641	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
642	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
643	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
644	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
645	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
646	
647	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
648	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
649	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
650	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
651	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
652	
653	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
654	
655	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
656	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
657	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
658	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
659	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
660	
661	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
662	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
663	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
664	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
665	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
666	
667	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
668	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
669	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
670	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
671	
672	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
673	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
674	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
675	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
676	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
677	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
678	
679	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
680	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
681	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
682	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
683	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
684	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
685	
686	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
687	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
688	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
689	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/

693	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
694	
695	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
696	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
697	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
698	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
699

700	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
701	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
702	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
703	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
704	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
705	
706	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
707	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
708	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
709	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
710	
711	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
712	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
713	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
714	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
715	
716	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
717	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
718	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
719	
720	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
721	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
722	element is finitely presented), bridged through
723	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
724	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
725	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
726	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
727	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
728	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
729	      inferInstance)
730	
731	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
732	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
733	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
734	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
735	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
736	
737	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
738	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
739	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
740	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
741

743	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
744	  unfold GmRing
745	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
746	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
747	
748	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
749	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)

751	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
752	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
753	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
754	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
755	  infer_instance

770	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once

813	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is

830	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
831	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))

882	          (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
883	        (GmRing kbar)
884	  | ⟨1, _⟩ =>

887	      pullback.congrHom

890	      pullbackSpecIso kbar

892	          (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))

491	/- Let's consider the following diagram of pullbacks
492	```
493	(X ×[Z] Y) ×[Y] W --snd--> W
494	    |                      |
495	   fst                     g'
496	    v                      v
497	 (X ×[Z] Y) --- snd --->   Y
498	    |                      |
499	   fst                     g
500	    v                      v
501	    X -------- f --------> Z
502	
503	```
504	In this section we show that `(X ×[Z] Y) ×[Y] W ≅ X ×[Z] W`.
505	-/

/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).

Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
        (algebraMap kbar
          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
  ext r
  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
      algebraMap kbar
        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
    rfl
  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
      MvPolynomial.C r :=
    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
  rw [← hinv, hround]
  rfl

input.old_string: /-- **Cocycle agreement for `gmScalingP1_chart`** on intersections of `(gmScalingP1_cover).f`.
input.new_string:
/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.

This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).

Proof chain (per analogist 10-step recipe):
1. `awayι_comp_PLB_hom`: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar Away)`.
2. `Spec.map_comp` + `CommRingCat.ofHom_comp`: merge the two `Spec.map`s on LHS into
   `Spec.map (chart_map.comp (algebraMap kbar Away_i))`.
3. `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq` +
   `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`: identify `chart_map.comp (algebraMap kbar Away_i)` with
   `algebraMap kbar (Away_i ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`.
4. `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base`: replace `pullbackSpecIso ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar tensor)`
   with `pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)`.
5. `pullback.congrHom_hom` + `pullback.lift_fst`: cancel the congrHom.
6. `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst`: collapse the pasting iso.
7. `pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst`: swap fst ↔ snd.
8. `pullback.condition` + `Over.tensorObj_hom`: align RHS.
9. `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (reverse): RHS reformulation. -/
private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
  -- RHS: (cover).f i ≫ pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom ≫ PLB.hom (by Over.tensorObj_hom).
  -- (cover).f i = pullback.fst (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (cover.openCover.f i).
  -- By pullback.condition, this composes with pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom = snd ≫ cover.openCover.f i.
  -- cover.openCover.f i = Proj.awayι _ ((![X 0, X 1]) i) _ _ = Proj.awayι _ X_i _ _ after fin_cases.
  -- So RHS = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ Proj.awayι _ X_i _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i).
  -- LHS chart: gmScalingP1_chart i = (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso i).hom ≫ Spec.map chart_map ≫ Proj.awayι.
  -- After awayι_comp_PLB_hom: LHS = iso.hom ≫ Spec.map chart_map ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)
  --                              = iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (chart_map.comp (algMap kbar Away_i))  (via Spec.map_comp)
  -- The composed ring map equals algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗[kbar] GmRing) by the kbar-algebra preservation.
  -- Then pullbackSpecIso_hom_base + congrHom_hom + pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst +
  -- pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst collapse iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar tensor) to
  -- pullback.snd ((pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom)) (Proj.awayι _ X_i _ _) ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i).
  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
  -- Rebracket so we can apply `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` to the right-tail.
  rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (MvPolynomial.X i)
        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)]
  -- Now merge the two consecutive Spec.maps on LHS.
  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
  -- The merged ring map: chart_map.comp (algMap kbar Away_i) = algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing).
  -- This follows from homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap + eval₂Hom_C.
  have hcomp :
      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (TensorProduct kbar
            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
              (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) (GmRing kbar)))
          (fun _ : Unit =>
            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
                (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
                (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
              (match i with
               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
      (algebraMap kbar
        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
    algebraMap kbar (TensorProduct kbar
      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) (GmRing kbar)) := by
    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap,
      MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom _ = CommRingCat.ofHom _ from
    congrArg CommRingCat.ofHom hcomp]
  -- LHS now: iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing)).
  -- Unfold the iso composition.
  unfold gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso
  -- The match on `i` requires fin_cases:
  fin_cases i
  · -- i = 0
    simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc]
    -- Apply pullbackSpecIso_hom_base on the tail.
    rw [pullbackSpecIso_hom_base]
    -- Then congrHom_hom + pullback.lift_fst, pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst,
    -- pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst, pullback.condition.
    -- RHS unfold via Over.tensorObj_hom.
    show _ = pullback.fst _ _ ≫ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _ ≫ _
    -- Now collapse via simp.
    simp only [pullback.congrHom_hom, pullback.lift_fst_assoc, pullback.lift_fst,
      Category.assoc, pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc,
      pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst_assoc, Category.id_comp,
      Over.tensorObj_hom]
    -- After collapsing, we should have pullback.snd ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_0)
    -- = (cover).f 0 ≫ pullback.fst _ _ ≫ PLB.hom on each side. Use awayι_comp_PLB_hom + pullback.condition.
    rw [show (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f ⟨0, by decide⟩ ≫
        pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom =
      pullback.snd (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f ⟨0, by decide⟩) ≫
        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f ⟨0, by decide⟩ from
      pullback.condition]
    -- Then rewrite cover.openCover.f 0 = Proj.awayι _ X_0 _ _, then awayι_comp_PLB_hom.
    sorry
  · -- i = 1 (symmetric)
    sorry
